title: What to Do When a Parent Refuses to Stop Driving
summary: When persuasion isn't working, families do have some real options — though none of them are easy or fast.
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: What to Do When a Parent Refuses to Stop Driving | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: When persuasion isn't working, families do have some real options — though none of them are easy or fast.
whatYouNeedToKnow: A doctor can sometimes report safety concerns to the DMV, depending on the state.
Some families quietly limit access to keys or the car as a last resort.
This is a genuinely hard situation, and disagreement among family members is common.

paragraphs: Rules vary by state, but in many places a doctor can submit a report to the DMV recommending a re-evaluation of driving privileges, which can carry more official weight than a family request alone. It's worth asking their doctor directly whether this is an option.

As a last resort, some families make the car less accessible — parking it elsewhere, disabling it temporarily, or having a frank conversation about not replacing keys. These steps understandably feel difficult and are usually a final option after other approaches haven't worked, not a first move.

nextSteps: Ask the doctor about state-specific reporting options.
Get the full family aligned before taking any unilateral action, if possible.
Have alternative transportation fully ready before removing driving access.
disclaimer: DMV reporting rules and processes vary significantly by state and are general information here, not legal advice. Confirm the actual process with your state's DMV or an elder law attorney before relying on it.
